name: Baked Alaska & Variations
recipeInstructions:
You can have Baked Alaska on short notice if you keep cake and ice cream on hand in your freezer.
1. Preheat oven to 450°F. For the cake base, choose one of Alaskas below. Set the cake base on brown paper (½ inch larger than the cake) on a cookie sheet.
2. Make meringue: With an egg beater or electric mixer, beat 3 egg whites until they form stiff peaks when the beater is raised. Slowly add 6 tablespoons granulated sugar, beating until stiff and glossy.
3. Quickly fill or top the cake base with 1 quart very firm ice cream as directed below. Quickly cover the ice cream and base completely with meringue. If desired, sprinkle with slivered almonds, shaved chocolate, or shredded coconut. Bake for 4 to 5 minutes, or until delicate brown.
4. Remove Alaska from the oven at once. Slip 2 spatulas between the Baked Alaska and paper. Transfer Alaska to a chilled serving dish. Garnish with canned peach slices, berries, etc. Serve immediately.
P.S. To serve ablaze, pour a bit of lemon extract on 3 sugar cubes; set on meringue; light; carry to table.
Surprise Alaska: Make a trough in a 9-inch tube sponge cake, leaving a ¾-inch-thick shell (see cutting directions for Frozen Angel, above). Fill the trough with ice cream.
Igloo Alaska: Use a layer of bakers' sponge cake. Pile ice cream on top, leaving
Brownie Alaska: Use a panful of uncut brownies. Top brownies with brick ice cream.
